title: Codebase Recovery — Lock conventions, install drift gates, consolidate SQL, normalise names
scope:
Execute the locked plan in docs/c_c_the_state_of_the_codebase.md — drag the codebase from its current state (SQL scattered across 56 of 137 backend files, inconsistent table/route naming, CI gates that only fire on PR-to-main) into a shape a DBA or fresh engineer can read at first glance.

Built from four parallel Opus audits run 2026-05-13 — not from memory. Audit findings: 42 backend packages, 32,877 lines non-test Go, 461 raw SQL string literals embedded in service files, zero sql.go files, 10 packages touching >1 database, 22 cross-DB function paths (5 high-risk).

Plan is structured as 7 phases (Phase 0–6) with hard stop gates between every phase. Every commit reversible. No phase begins without explicit user confirmation. Naming conventions locked in §2 of the master doc; any deviation requires a register entry. The master doc itself (c_c_the_state_of_the_codebase.md) is the authoritative reference — this plan card tracks execution against it.
value:
Recovers a codebase the owner does not currently trust enough to release under his own name. After completion: any engineer or DBA cloning the repo can predict every file's location, find every SQL string in one file per package, and trust that docs/c_c_db_routing.md matches the code. Future table moves take minutes, not weeks. The drift-prevention lints installed in Phase 1 keep the rest of the work — and every future feature — from re-introducing the same patterns.

Removes the structural shame from the project. Lets the owner ship.
implementation_plan: Phase 0 — Codify conventions: write docs/c_c_naming_conventions.md as a leaf doc capturing every rule from §2 of the master plan (Go packages, tables, routes, file layout, migrations). Update CLAUDE.md index with a one-line pointer. NO CODE CHANGES. Stop gate: user reviews the conventions doc before any code change.

Phase 1 — Install drift-prevention lints BEFORE the rewrite: five new lints added to dev/scripts/ — lint:sql-in-sqlfile-only (with wide allow-list seeded from current state), lint:no-empty-route-block, lint:exemption-ratchet, lint:deferral-needs-td-id, lint:package-naming-convention. New CI workflow tests.yml running them all on push (not just PR-to-main). Stop gate: all five lints pass against HEAD.

Phase 2 — sql.go consolidation, ONE PACKAGE AT A TIME, in this order: topology (post-tonight, includes orgdesign → topology rename), auth, users, roles, permissions, addressables, nav, flows, webhooks, timeboxsprints + timeboxreleases, workspaces, tenantsettings, fields, searchworker, errorsreport, libraryreleases, librarydb, portfolio, artefactitemsv2 (renamed deferred to Phase 4), portfoliomodels (saga, last). Per-package shape: create sql.go, move every SQL literal to a named const, update functions to reference constants, build clean, run tests, smoke one route, commit, shrink lint allow-list. Stop gate after EVERY package.

Phase 3 — Per-DB migration directories: git mv db/schema/ → db/mmff_vector/schema/, db/artefacts_schema/ → db/vector_artefacts/schema/, db/library_schema/ → db/mmff_library/schema/. Down migrations move with them. Update backend/cmd/migrate/main.go, c_db-backup.md, backup-on-push.sh, any tooling that walks db/. Stop gate: go run ./cmd/migrate -dry-run shows zero pending against each DB.

Phase 4 — Naming-convention sweep, ONE RENAME AT A TIME: 4.1 Go package renames (artefactitemsv2 → artefactitems, wsperms → workspacepermissions, entityrefs → polymorphicrefs, dbcheck → dbinvariants, models → roletypes, messages → usermessages, tenantsettings → tenantmasterrecord). 4.2 Table renames per-DB (topology_view_state → topology_view_states, audit_log → audit_logs, artefacts_search_outbox → artefact_search_outbox, master_record_portfolio → master_record_portfolios, portfolio_templates → portfolio_models, master_record_workspaces → workspaces, plus the legacy drops). 4.3 Route renames (/workspace/{id}/* → /workspaces/{id}/*, /portfolio → /portfolios, /errors/report → POST /error-reports, /admin/api-keys/issue → POST /admin/api-keys, etc.). Each rename is ONE commit. Stop gate per rename.

Phase 5 — Cross-DB writer hardening: each of the 5 high-risk cross-DB writers (portfoliomodels.Orchestrator.Adopt, portfoliomodels.DevResetHandler.MasterReset, artefactitemsv2.Service.CreateWorkItem, libraryreleases.Handler.Ack, errorsreport.Handler.Report) gets an explicit *_crossdb_test.go regression test plus a partial-failure scenario doc comment. File TD entries for any actual partial-failure bugs uncovered. Add lint:cross-db-writer-test. Stop gate per writer.

Phase 6 — Documentation pass: regenerate docs/c_c_db_routing.md from code reality, update docs/c_schema.md, finalise docs/c_c_naming_conventions.md, reduce CLAUDE.md index to one-line-only entries per the standing rule. Stop gate: user reads the regenerated docs.

DOCS: CLAUDE.md — index entry for the conventions doc; index normalisation in Phase 6

DEV: dev/scripts/lint_exemption_ratchet.sh — NEW (Phase 1)

Legacy mmff_vector.sprints table — superseded by vector_artefacts.timebox_sprints, dropped (Phase 4.2)
Legacy obj_* table prefix family — renamed or dropped as last readers migrate (Phase 4.2)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: docs/c_c_naming_conventions.md exists, is referenced from CLAUDE.md, and codifies every rule from §2 of c_c_the_state_of_the_codebase.md (packages, tables, routes, file layout, migrations).
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Manual review: open the file, cross-check against §2 of the master doc; CLAUDE.md index line present.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Five drift-prevention lints (sql-in-sqlfile-only, no-empty-route-block, exemption-ratchet, deferral-needs-td-id, package-naming-convention) all pass against HEAD with allow-lists seeded from current state. tests.yml CI workflow runs on every push and includes npm test + npx tsc --noEmit + go test ./... + go vet ./...
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: npm run lint:sql-in-sqlfile-only && npm run lint:no-empty-route-block && npm run lint:exemption-ratchet && npm run lint:package-naming-convention all exit 0; .github/workflows/tests.yml exists with the four jobs; first push to a feature branch triggers the workflow.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every backend/internal/<pkg>/ directory containing SQL has a sql.go file holding all SQL literals as named constants (sqlVerbResource form). No SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE literal exists outside a sql.go file under backend/internal/. lint:sql-in-sqlfile-only passes with an empty allow-list.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: find backend/internal -name 'sql.go' | wc -l matches the count of SQL-touching packages; grep -rE '(SELECT |INSERT INTO|UPDATE [a-z_]+ SET|DELETE FROM)' backend/internal --include='*.go' --exclude='*_test.go' --exclude='sql.go' returns zero hits; lint allow-list file is empty.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Migrations live under db/<dbname>/schema/ and db/<dbname>/schema/down/ for each of mmff_vector, vector_artefacts, mmff_library. backend/cmd/migrate/main.go reads from the new paths. go run ./cmd/migrate -dry-run -db <each> reports zero pending migrations on dev.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: ls db/mmff_vector/schema | wc -l matches the pre-move count for db/schema; equivalent for vector_artefacts and mmff_library; go run ./cmd/migrate -db mmff_vector -dry-run exits 0 with 'up to date'.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Package directory naming follows §2.1: no version suffix without a coexisting peer; no insider abbreviations from the rename list. artefactitemsv2 → artefactitems landed; the five other Phase 4.1 renames landed; import-path fan-out updated everywhere.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: ls backend/internal/ shows no 'artefactitemsv2', 'wsperms', 'entityrefs', 'dbcheck', 'models' (as a package name for role types), 'messages'; grep -r 'artefactitemsv2' backend/ returns zero hits; lint:package-naming-convention passes with no exemptions.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Table names follow §2.2: plural collections, snake_case, no obj_*/o_* legacy prefixes among LIVE tables, no inconsistent singular/plural pairs within a family. The full Phase-4.2 rename list completed.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: psql -d each DB -c '\dt' shows the renamed tables; the SQL files in db/<dbname>/schema/ matching CREATE TABLE statements use only the canonical names; lint:table-prefix-allowlist (Phase 4 deliverable, optional) passes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: HTTP routes follow §2.3: plural, REST-canonical verbs, no /workspace/{id} singular, no internal terms (master_record) in external routes. Phase-4.3 rename list completed. Old routes either removed or kept with 410 Gone / 301 redirect for one release cycle.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: curl http://localhost:5100/_site/workspaces/<id>/fields returns 401 (route exists); curl http://localhost:5100/_site/workspace/<id>/fields returns 410 or 301. check_routes.sh --all passes 0 errors.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The 5 high-risk cross-DB writers (Adopt, MasterReset, CreateWorkItem, Ack, Report) each have a *_crossdb_test.go regression test exercising the partial-failure scenario; the file header comment documents the partial-failure window. lint:cross-db-writer-test passes.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: ls backend/internal/portfoliomodels/*_crossdb_test.go AND adopt.go, dev_reset.go header comments name the partial-failure window. Equivalent for the other 4 writers. lint:cross-db-writer-test exits 0.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: docs/c_c_db_routing.md matches code reality post-rewrite — every Go service is listed with its pool, DB, tables. docs/c_schema.md lists every live table under the correct DB. CLAUDE.md index entries are all single-line per the authoring rule.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Manual review: open c_c_db_routing.md, pick three services at random, confirm each line matches the running code by grepping main.go. CLAUDE.md has no multi-line index entries.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The five completion tests in §6 of the master doc all hold: package directory layout predictable; conventions doc covers every name; migrate dry-run zero pending; npm run api:check + npm test + go test all pass; c_c_db_routing.md matches code.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Run the five checks in §6 verbatim; record output; all pass.

risks.risk: Phase 2.20 (portfoliomodels sql.go consolidation) is the saga with 51 SQL strings across multiple sub-files. Higher chance of mistake during the move; could break adoption flow.
risks.mitigation: Defer to absolute last package in Phase 2. By that point, 19 prior consolidations have proven the pattern. Phase 5's cross-DB regression tests cover the adoption-saga partial-failure paths anyway. Phase 2.20 commits ONE function at a time, not the whole package in one shot.

risks.risk: Phase 4.2 table renames against vector_artefacts touch live data in dev. A botched ALTER TABLE could lock the table or break referential integrity.
risks.mitigation: Every rename has a paired DOWN migration. Run UP, smoke-test, run DOWN on dev as a dry-run before pushing. Use ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO ... (cheap, atomic, no data rewrite). For tables involved in foreign keys or polymorphic refs, audit cross_db_canary.sh expectations before AND after the rename.

risks.risk: Phase 4.3 route renames break external API consumers if any exist. /samantha/v2 is the public surface; renames there are public-API breaking changes.
risks.mitigation: /_site routes are internal — safe to rename in-place with frontend updates in same commit. /samantha/v2 routes get the old route kept with 410 Gone OR 301 redirect for one release cycle; renames flagged in openapi-v2.yaml with deprecated: true; oasdiff breaking gate runs on every push (Phase 1 + TD-API-004 fix).

risks.risk: Phase 1 lint:sql-in-sqlfile-only with a wide allow-list might be tempting to treat as 'pass' when really it just lists everything as exempt — the lint passes vacuously.
risks.mitigation: The lint's exit-0 condition is 'every non-allow-listed file has zero SQL'. The allow-list is seeded by enumerating current SQL-containing files; lint passes immediately because every offender IS allow-listed. Phase 2 shrinks the allow-list one package per commit — the lint stays green and the allow-list ratchet (lint:exemption-ratchet) catches any attempt to add a new file to it.

risks.risk: Phase 3 (per-DB migration dirs) might confuse backend/cmd/migrate's schema_migrations tracking if any migration filename is duplicated across the old and new paths during the cutover.
risks.mitigation: git mv preserves file content. backend/cmd/migrate reads filename only (not path) for schema_migrations matching. Verify post-move that go run ./cmd/migrate -dry-run reports zero pending; if it reports the moved files as 'new', backfill schema_migrations names match what's already recorded.

risks.risk: Scope creep: 'while we're in there' temptation to fix unrelated bugs during a sql.go consolidation, expanding a 30-minute commit into a 3-hour debug session.
risks.mitigation: Rules of Engagement §7.4 in the master doc is absolute: stop gates are absolute. Any deviation files a TD entry first and asks for direction. Improvising without a register entry is what got us here. Conventions doc + feedback_deferrals_register.md memory rule enforce this.

risks.risk: Phase 5 cross-DB regression tests need the SSH tunnel + live dev DBs to run; CI may not have access.
risks.mitigation: Use the same skip-on-unreachable pattern existing integration tests use (t.Skipf when pool ping fails). Tests are green locally + the skip path is documented; CI workflow tolerates skipped tests as long as compile passes.

references.label: DB routing — service → pool → DB → tables (will be regenerated in Phase 6)
PLA-0023 — mmff_vector → vector_artefacts cutover (parent thread of substrate work)
PLA-0026 — Adoption saga across three DBs (Phase 5 hardening target)
PLA-0030 — v1 → v2 API cutover (overlaps Phase 4.3 route renames)
PLA-0039 — Transport segregation (BFF vs public) — relevant to Phase 4.3 route placement
